name: LHCb prompt A' -> mu+mu-
observable: epsilon vs mA' -visible A'->mu+mu-
available_observables: m_A' from model
conventions: LHCb uses epsilon^2 convention. BR(A'->mu+mu-) ~ 1 for mA'>2m_mu if A' only decays to visible SM
applicability: Applies to entire 1-sigma mA' range (0.31-0.67 GeV). If BR(A'->mu+mu-) ~ 1, excludes epsilon > few x 1e-4. But dark photon with dark Higgs rho opens invisible channel A'->rho+phistar - actual BR depends on unknown couplings
source_arxiv: Search for A'->mu+mu- Decays, LHCb

name: BaBar invisible A' monophoton
observable: epsilon vs mA' - invisible A' decay
available_observables: m_A' from model
conventions: BaBar uses epsilon convention. Bound applies to invisible decay: BR(A'->invisible) ~ 1
applicability: Applies if A' decays invisibly (e.g. to dark sector particles). Excludes epsilon > few x 1e-4 for mA' ~0.3-0.7 GeV. However, both visible (to SM) and invisible (to rho/A' pairs) channels may be open
source_arxiv: Search for Invisible Decays of a Dark Photon at BaBar

name: NA64 invisible dark photon (electron beam)
observable: epsilon vs mA' - invisible A'->DM
available_observables: m_A' from model
conventions: NA64 uses epsilon convention. 90% CL limits. A' produced via dark bremsstrahlung. Invisible decay required.
applicability: Applies only to mA' < 350 MeV lower edge of 1-sigma region. NA64 covers 214-350 MeV partially overlapping the 1-sigma mA' lower end (0.31 GeV). Excludes epsilon > few x 1e-4 for detectable A' produced in electron beam. Sensitivity drops sharply above 350 MeV.
source_arxiv: Search for Light Dark Matter with NA64 (2023)

name: BBN dark photon decay constraint
observable: tau_A' < 0.2 s constraint from rho'/rho_tot at BBN
available_observables: m_A' from model; no abundance computed
conventions: Coy et al. 2025 convention. BBN requires tau' < 0.2 s. For m_A' ~ 300-700 MeV, epsilon > 10^{-9}-10^{-8} required. Bound is lower limit on epsilon.
applicability: Applies to A' that decays to SM. For m_A' ~ 300-700 MeV, requires epsilon > 10^{-8} for decay before BBN. This gives a lower bound rather than an upper bound. However, if the dark sector has a small abundance, this may be relaxed.
source_arxiv: Light from darkness: history of a hot dark sector (Coy et al. 2025)

name: BBN dark scalar (rho) decay constraint
observable: rho lifetime and decay to e+e-/hadrons vs BBN
available_observables: m_rho from model; no abundance or branching fractions computed
missing_observables: rho lifetime, BR(rho->e+e-), BR(rho->hadrons), rho freeze-in yield
units: lifetime in seconds, mixing angle dimensionless
conventions: BBN constrains particles decaying with tau > 0.1 s. For m_rho=50-100 MeV, lifetime is controlled by scalar mixing angle sin^2(theta) = lambda_HPhi * v_dark * v_SM / m_h^2
applicability: For m_rho=50-100 MeV, decays to e+e- dominate. BBN excludes theta > 10^{-6}-10^{-7} (tau < 200 s regime) and CMB excludes 10^{-11} < theta < 10^{-6}. Full constraint requires computing rho lifetime, branching ratios, and abundance.
source_arxiv: Cosmological beam dump: constraints on dark scalars mixed with the Higgs boson

name: CMB energy injection from decaying rho
observable: CMB anisotropy constraints on decaying particles
available_observables: m_rho from model; no lifetime or BR computed
missing_observables: rho lifetime, BR(rho->e+e-), rho freeze-in yield
conventions: CMB constrains energy injection at z < 1100 from decaying particles. For m_rho ~ 50-100 MeV with e+e- final state, excludes theta > 10^{-10} for tau_rho > 10^{13} s.
applicability: Range theta ~ 10^{-11}-10^{-10} excluded by Planck CMB power spectrum for m_rho~50-100 MeV decaying to e+e- with lifetime > 10^{13} s. Requires abundance computation.
source_arxiv: Cosmological beam dump: constraints on dark scalars mixed with the Higgs boson

name: Delta N_eff from dark radiation
observable: Number of additional relativistic dofs at BBN/CMB
available_observables: m_A', m_rho from model; xi=1 assumed
conventions: CMB Planck 2018: N_eff = 2.99 +/- 0.17, Delta N_eff < 0.3 at 95% CL. BBN: N_eff = 2.88 +/- 0.27.
applicability: If xi=1, at T>>masses, dark sector has 4 bosonic dofs (A':3, rho:1) giving Delta N_eff = 4 * (8/7) * (xi)^4 * (g_dark/g_gamma) ~ 0.57 for xi=1. This already exceeds Planck 95% CL bound. However, xi=1 is assumed by FOPT backend - constraint depends on whether dark sector is truly thermalized with SM.
source_arxiv: Planck 2018 results. VI. Cosmological parameters

name: Perturbativity bound (g_D)
available_observables: g_D=0.509-0.545 from 1-sigma
conventions: Standard perturbativity requirement for gauge coupling
applicability: Satisfied: g_D^2/(4*pi) = 0.082-0.095, well below unity.
source_arxiv: Perturbativity is a standard theory criterion

name: Coleman-Weinberg consistency (beta_lambda>0)
observable: beta_lambda = 3*g_D^4/(8*pi^2)
conventions: CW mechanism requires beta_lambda > 0 for radiative symmetry breaking
applicability: Satisfied: beta_lambda = 0.0025-0.0034 > 0. Boson-dominated CW consistent.
source_arxiv: Standard CW consistency condition

name: Scalar mixing from Higgs signal strength
observable: BR(h->invisible) or BR(h->beyond SM)
available_observables: m_rho from model; no lambda_HPhi value
conventions: Higgs signal strength constraints: BR(h-> exotic) < 10-20% at 95% CL. Mixing angle sin(theta) < 0.12-0.28 for m_rho < 40 GeV.
applicability: Constraint depends on lambda_HPhi. For m_rho < m_h/2, h->rho+rho is possible. For m_rho=50-100 MeV, rho is light enough. The exotic Higgs width constraint gives lambda_HPhi < 0.01-0.1 depending on rho mass and mixing.
source_arxiv: Combining LEP and LHC to bound the Higgs Width

name: Beam dump constraints on dark scalar (CHARM, PS191, LSND)
observable: Scalar production in K decays or proton beam dump, detection via e+e- decays
available_observables: m_rho from model; no mixing angle or BR
conventions: PS191 (Gorbunov 2021): excludes sin^2(theta) ~ 10^{-7}-3x10^{-7} for m_rho=100-150 MeV. CHARM: excludes sin^2(theta) ~ 10^{-7}-10^{-6} similar range.
applicability: For m_rho=50-100 MeV, PS191 and CHARM cover only the upper end (100 MeV). At 50-71 MeV, these constraints may be weaker or absent. Kaon production threshold limits sensitivity.
source_arxiv: Constraints on light scalars from PS191 results

name: Stellar cooling (RGB/HB) from A'
observable: Stellar energy loss rate from A' production
required_inputs: m_A', epsilon, plasma frequency in stellar cores
available_observables: m_A' from model
conventions: Resonant A' production in stellar cores occurs when m_A' ~ plasma frequency. For m_A' ~ keV, epsilon < 10^{-15}.
applicability: Not applicable: m_A' = 300-700 MeV >> thermal plasma frequencies in stellar cores (~keV). Off-resonant production is exponentially suppressed for m_A' >> T_star.
source_arxiv: Constraining dark photons with self-consistent simulations of globular cluster stars (Dolan et al. 2023)

name: SN1987A cooling from A'
observable: SN1987A neutrino signal duration from A' cooling
required_inputs: m_A', epsilon, production rate in SN core
available_observables: m_A' from model
conventions: Traditional SN1987A cooling bound applies for m_A' < 200 MeV. New gain-layer constraint (Caputo 2025) strongest at 0.1-0.4 MeV.
applicability: Not applicable: m_A'=300-700 MeV > 200 MeV upper bound for SN cooling constraints. For m_A' this heavy, production in SN core is suppressed by Boltzmann factor exp(-m_A'/T) with T_core ~ 30-60 MeV.
source_arxiv: Cooling the Shock: New Supernova Constraints on Dark Photons (Caputo et al. 2025)

name: Direct detection (XENONnT, LZ) dark photon absorption
observable: DM-electron scattering / dark photon absorption in LXe
available_observables: m_A' from model
conventions: XENONnT constrains absorption of dark photons with mass < keV via electron recoils.
applicability: Not applicable: m_A' = 300-700 MeV >> keV range accessible to direct detection via absorption. Direct detection probes sub-MeV dark photon DM.
source_arxiv: XENONnT Search for Light Dark Matter (2025)

name: Delta N_eff from residual dark dofs after PT
observable: Effective number of relativistic species after phase transition and reheating
available_observables: T_reh from FOPT benchmarks, alpha, beta/H from PTArcade
missing_observables: Residual dark particle abundance after reheating
conventions: CMB Planck 2018: Delta N_eff < 0.3 at 95% CL. BBN: Delta N_eff < 0.4.
applicability: Very strong supercooling (alpha ~ 10^11) means the dark sector is very cold after PT. Reheating temperature T_reh can be estimated from FOPT parameters. The residual abundance of dark sector particles depends on reheat dynamics and needs calculation.

name: Theory: Classical scale invariance stability (no tree-level mass term)
observable: Consistency of classical scale invariance assumption
available_observables: Model has no tree-level mass term
conventions: Model is classically scale invariant at tree level. No dimensionful parameters.
applicability: Confirmed: Lagrangian contains only |D_mu Phi|^2 - lambda |Phi|^4. No mass term.
source_arxiv: N/A

name: BBN: additional relativistic degrees of freedom at T~MeV
observable: Delta N_eff from additional relativistic dofs during BBN
required_inputs: T_dark/T_SM, effective dofs in dark sector
available_observables: m_A', m_rho from model; xi=1 assumed
missing_observables: Evolution of dark sector temperature through PT and reheating
conventions: Planck+BBN: Delta N_eff < 0.4 at BBN. With xi=1, dark sector 4 bosonic dofs give Delta N_eff ~ 0.57.
applicability: For xi=1, the dark sector dofs exceed BBN bound. However, after the supercooled PT, the dark sector reheats to T_reh, and residual abundance of massive particles may be negligible. Need to compute actual energy density in dark sector at BBN.

name: NA62 dark photon visible decay A'->mu+mu-
observable: epsilon vs mA' from NA62 beam dump mu+mu- search
available_observables: m_A' from model
conventions: NA62 (JHEP 09, 035, 2023): excludes 215-550 MeV range for visible A'->mu+mu- decays. 1.4e17 protons on dump.
applicability: Partially overlaps 1-sigma region at lower end (mA'=0.31 GeV). NA62 excludes epsilon ~ 10^{-6}-10^{-5} for m_A'=215-550 MeV. Upper end of 1-sigma region (0.55-0.67 GeV) is not covered.
source_arxiv: Search for dark photon decays to mu+mu- at NA62

name: KLOE-2 dark photon constraints
observable: epsilon from e+e- -> U gamma, U -> pi+pi-
available_observables: m_A' from model
missing_observables: KLOE-2 probes m_A' in rho-omega mass region (~550 MeV)
conventions: KLOE-2 constraints focus on mA' ~ 527-568 MeV (rho-omega interference region)
applicability: Not directly applicable: KLOE-2 probes a narrow mass window (rho-omega region). Our 1-sigma mA' range (0.31-0.67 GeV) includes this region, but KLOE-2 constraints are model-dependent (assume A' couples to quarks as dark photon) and less stringent than LHCb.
source_arxiv: KLOE-2 dark photon search in pi+pi- gamma final state

name: Supernova gamma-ray constraints on dark photon
observable: Prompt and diffuse gamma rays from SN1987A from A' decays
available_observables: m_A' from model
missing_observables: m_A' > 200 MeV production in SN is suppressed
conventions: Gamma-ray constraints from SN1987A apply when A' is produced in SN core and decays outside, producing gamma rays.
applicability: Not applicable: for m_A'=300-700 MeV, production in SN core is Boltzmann-suppressed by exp(-m_A'/T_core) which is tiny (T_core ~ 30-60 MeV).
source_arxiv: High-energy neutrino signals from supernova explosions (Syvolap & Ruchayskiy 2024)

name: Dark rho production via Higgs mixing at LEP/LHC
observable: Exotic Higgs decays h -> rho+rho, rare meson decays (B, K)
available_observables: m_rho from model; no mixing value
conventions: CMS EXO-24-034: BR(h->SS) < 10^{-4} for m_S=0.4-2 GeV. For m_rho<280 MeV, rho->e+e-/pipi/KK channels are open.
applicability: For m_rho=50-100 MeV, sensitivity from CMS h->SS search ends at m_S=0.4 GeV (our rho is lighter). Kaon decay experiments (NA62, E949) provide complementary coverage for m_rho < m_K - m_pi. Need kaon-production calculations.
source_arxiv: CMS search for light scalars via Higgs portal (2025)

name: Structure formation constraints on warm dark matter
observable: Lyman-alpha forest constraints on light thermal relics
required_inputs: Particle velocity dispersion, mass, production mechanism
available_observables: m_rho from model; rho is not a DM candidate
units: Structure formation constraints apply to DM candidates.
conventions: Not applicable: rho is not a dark matter candidate in this model (the model has no DM candidate). The A' and rho are unstable and decay to SM particles via portals.
applicability: status:"no DM candidate"
tested_subregion: Model has no fermionic DM candidate
source_arxiv: low

name: Dark scalar freeze-in and relic overproduction
observable: Yield Y_S from freeze-in via Higgs mixing
available_observables: m_rho from model; no mixing value
conventions: For sin^2(theta) > 10^{-6}, scalar thermalizes and follows freeze-out abundance (overcloses universe). For sin^2(theta) < 10^{-6}, freeze-in yield Y_S ~ 3e11*theta^2.
applicability: Constraint: veto sin^2(theta) > 10^{-6} to avoid thermalization and overproduction. For 10^{-12} < sin^2(theta) < 10^{-7}, freeze-in produces a subdominant population that must decay before BBN/CMB. The decoupling temperature of the dark sector from SM depends on portal couplings and must be computed to verify xi=1 assumption.
source_arxiv: Cosmological beam dump: constraints on dark scalars mixed with the Higgs boson (Fradette et al. 2018)
